prose:
**Oxygen frontier-orbital result** — Property: HOMO–LUMO gap from a frontier-orbitals single-point calculation. Method used: DFT tier `standard`, explicitly `b3lyp/def2-tzvp`, in PySCF. Geometry: user-supplied fixed XYZ file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/oxygen/oxygen.xyz` (2 atoms; not geometry-optimized in this task). Environment: gas phase (`--solvent` omitted). Charge 0, multiplicity 3. SCF converged: true, in 7 cycles. Engine integrity trustworthy verdict: true. Provenance: computed.

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = **5.2880586 eV**.

Additional returned values: HOMO = -8.8739616 eV; LUMO = -3.5859031 eV; total electronic energy = -150.38872 hartree = -4092.2855 eV. This is a Kohn–Sham orbital gap, not an excitation energy.

Warnings: no warnings were reported by the tool result.

Reproducibility: chemkit CLI invocation returned by the tool was `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/oxygen/oxygen.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 3 --accept-defaults --stdout path --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpmvqpkjk2/live.json`. The result JSON path in that invocation is `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpmvqpkjk2/live.json`. The chemkit result returned here did not include a live `.out` log path, so I cannot truthfully name one beyond the returned JSON path.
